cards.front: What is an Adder in digital circuits?
cards.back: An adder is a combinational digital circuit that adds two or more binary numbers
An adder works by performing a series of binary additions, carrying over any overflow to the next bit position

cards.front: What is the main component of an Adder?
cards.back: The main component of an Adder is the full adder, which adds two binary numbers and produces a sum and carry output

cards.front: What is the role of Half Adders in an Adder?
cards.back: Half Adders are used to implement the full adder by combining two half adders to produce a sum and carry output

cards.front: What is the difference between Half Adder and Full Adder?
cards.back: The main difference between Half Adder and Full Adder is that Full Adder can handle a carry input, whereas Half Adder cannot
